title: cornus suecica
intro: Cornus suecica, also known as the Bog Elder or Spindle Tree, is a flowering plant native to northern Europe, particularly in acidic bogs and moors. Its medicinal properties lie in its berries, leaves, and bark, which contain anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cornus suecica include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as supporting immune function and reducing the risk of certain cancers. Herbal preparations of Cornus suecica are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and may be used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
